title: Improved Monitoring of Honey bee Colony Strength via Audio IoT Sensors, Modulation Tensorgrams and Recurrent Neural Networks
excerpt: Honey bees (Apis mellifera) play a crucial role in agriculture and ecosystem stability as key pollinators of crops and wild plants. As such, monitoring hive strength remotely with Internet of Things (IoT) sensors has become a crucial task. Previously, handcrafted features extracted from the modulation spectrum of audio IoT devices were shown to improve acoustic monitoring of colony strength. In this paper, we hypothesize that important discriminative information is present in the temporal dynamics of the modulation spectrum, but this information is discarded with prior methods. As such, we explore the use of a new modulation tensorgram where the time dimension is kept. This new representation is used as input to a convolutional neural network (CNN) and a convolutional recurrent deep neural networks (CRDNN). Using the public UrBAN dataset, which contains more than 3,000 hours of beehive audio recordings, we show that the proposed method improves both accuracy and cross-hive generalizability over prior benchmark methods, and the results further suggest improved robustness to noisy in-the-wild recording conditions. We use saliency maps and gradient-weighted class activation maps for explainability and show the importance of the modulation spectral temporal dynamics for the task at hand. Overall, our results suggest that accurate, generalizable, and robust acoustic monitoring of honey bee colony strength is possible.
content_html:
View PDF

Abstract:Honey bees (Apis mellifera) play a crucial role in agriculture and ecosystem stability as key pollinators of crops and wild plants. As such, monitoring hive strength remotely with Internet of Things (IoT) sensors has become a crucial task. Previously, handcrafted features extracted from the modulation spectrum of audio IoT devices were shown to improve acoustic monitoring of colony strength. In this paper, we hypothesize that important discriminative information is present in the temporal dynamics of the modulation spectrum, but this information is discarded with prior methods. As such, we explore the use of a new modulation tensorgram where the time dimension is kept. This new representation is used as input to a convolutional neural network (CNN) and a convolutional recurrent deep neural networks (CRDNN). Using the public UrBAN dataset, which contains more than 3,000 hours of beehive audio recordings, we show that the proposed method improves both accuracy and cross-hive generalizability over prior benchmark methods, and the results further suggest improved robustness to noisy in-the-wild recording conditions. We use saliency maps and gradient-weighted class activation maps for explainability and show the importance of the modulation spectral temporal dynamics for the task at hand. Overall, our results suggest that accurate, generalizable, and robust acoustic monitoring of honey bee colony strength is possible.
Subjects: Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)

blocks.html_en: Abstract:Honey bees (Apis mellifera) play a crucial role in agriculture and ecosystem stability as key pollinators of crops and wild plants. As such, monitoring hive strength remotely with Internet of Things (IoT) sensors has become a crucial task. Previously, handcrafted features extracted from the modulation spectrum of audio IoT devices were shown to improve acoustic monitoring of colony strength. In this paper, we hypothesize that important discriminative information is present in the temporal dynamics of the modulation spectrum, but this information is discarded with prior methods. As such, we explore the use of a new modulation tensorgram where the time dimension is kept. This new representation is used as input to a convolutional neural network (CNN) and a convolutional recurrent deep neural networks (CRDNN). Using the public UrBAN dataset, which contains more than 3,000 hours of beehive audio recordings, we show that the proposed method improves both accuracy and cross-hive generalizability over prior benchmark methods, and the results further suggest improved robustness to noisy in-the-wild recording conditions. We use saliency maps and gradient-weighted class activation maps for explainability and show the importance of the modulation spectral temporal dynamics for the task at hand. Overall, our results suggest that accurate, generalizable, and robust acoustic monitoring of honey bee colony strength is possible.

sentences.en: Abstract:Honey bees (Apis mellifera) play a crucial role in agriculture and ecosystem stability as key pollinators of crops and wild plants.
sentences.zh: 摘要：蜜蜂（Apis mellifera）作为农作物和野生植物的主要授粉媒介，在农业和生态系统稳定中发挥着至关重要的作用。

sentences.en: As such, monitoring hive strength remotely with Internet of Things (IoT) sensors has become a crucial task.
sentences.zh: 因此，利用物联网 (IoT) 传感器远程监控蜂巢强度已成为一项至关重要的任务。

sentences.en: Previously, handcrafted features extracted from the modulation spectrum of audio IoT devices were shown to improve acoustic monitoring of colony strength.
sentences.zh: 此前，从音频物联网设备的调制频谱中提取的手工特征被证明可以改善菌落强度的声学监测。

sentences.en: In this paper, we hypothesize that important discriminative information is present in the temporal dynamics of the modulation spectrum, but this information is discarded with prior methods.
sentences.zh: 在本文中，我们假设调制频谱的时间动态中存在重要的判别信息，但该信息被现有方法丢弃。

sentences.en: As such, we explore the use of a new modulation tensorgram where the time dimension is kept.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们探索使用保留时间维度的新调制张量图。

sentences.en: This new representation is used as input to a convolutional neural network (CNN) and a convolutional recurrent deep neural networks (CRDNN).
sentences.zh: 这种新的表示形式用作卷积神经网络（CNN）和卷积循环深度神经网络（CRDNN）的输入。

sentences.en: Using the public UrBAN dataset, which contains more than 3,000 hours of beehive audio recordings, we show that the proposed method improves both accuracy and cross-hive generalizability over prior benchmark methods, and the results further suggest improved robustness to noisy in-the-wild recording conditions.
sentences.zh: 使用包含超过 3,000 小时蜂巢音频记录的公共 UrBAN 数据集，我们表明所提出的方法比之前的基准方法提高了准确性和跨蜂巢泛化性，并且结果进一步表明提高了对野外噪声记录条件的鲁棒性。

sentences.en: We use saliency maps and gradient-weighted class activation maps for explainability and show the importance of the modulation spectral temporal dynamics for the task at hand.
sentences.zh: 我们使用显着图和梯度加权类激活图来实现可解释性，并展示调制谱时间动态对于当前任务的重要性。

sentences.en: Overall, our results suggest that accurate, generalizable, and robust acoustic monitoring of honey bee colony strength is possible.
sentences.zh: 总的来说，我们的结果表明，对蜂群强度进行准确、可推广和稳健的声学监测是可能的。

content_html_zh:
查看PDF

摘要：蜜蜂（Apis mellifera）作为农作物和野生植物的主要授粉媒介，在农业和生态系统稳定中发挥着至关重要的作用。 因此，利用物联网 (IoT) 传感器远程监控蜂巢强度已成为一项至关重要的任务。 此前，从音频物联网设备的调制频谱中提取的手工特征被证明可以改善菌落强度的声学监测。 在本文中，我们假设调制频谱的时间动态中存在重要的判别信息，但该信息被现有方法丢弃。 因此，我们探索使用保留时间维度的新调制张量图。 这种新的表示形式用作卷积神经网络（CNN）和卷积循环深度神经网络（CRDNN）的输入。 使用包含超过 3,000 小时蜂巢音频记录的公共 UrBAN 数据集，我们表明所提出的方法比之前的基准方法提高了准确性和跨蜂巢泛化性，并且结果进一步表明提高了对野外噪声记录条件的鲁棒性。 我们使用显着图和梯度加权类激活图来实现可解释性，并展示调制谱时间动态对于当前任务的重要性。 总的来说，我们的结果表明，对蜂群强度进行准确、可推广和稳健的声学监测是可能的。
科目： 音频和语音处理 (eess.AS)
引用为： arXiv:2607.20386 [eess.AS]
